description_long: This is a commercial-style upright vacuum cleaner with a robust, industrial design, featuring a tall, straight handle extending from a wide, low-profile base. The base is angular and slightly rounded at the corners, with a prominent raised section at the rear, likely housing the motor and dust collection system. A large, arched handle is integrated into the rear of the base, providing a secure grip for lifting or maneuvering. The main pole is metallic and cylindrical, with a visible clamp or latch mechanism partway up, possibly for adjusting the handle height. The surface finish is glossy and reflective, suggesting a hard plastic or metal construction. No visible power cord or brand logo is present, and the wheels are not clearly seen from any angle.
description: A glossy, upright vacuum cleaner with a tall handle and a wide, angular base, featuring a prominent rear grip and industrial styling.
description_view_0: Front view shows the tall handle, wide base, and central rear grip, with the main pole and clamp clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlights the raised rear section of the base and the curvature of the handle, with the clamp and pole offset.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the arched handle integrated into the base and the symmetrical design of the main body.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view reveals the base's depth and the rear housing, with the handle and clamp visible from a different perspective.
attribute_handle_design: Tall, straight cylindrical handle with a clamp for adjustment and a large arched grip integrated into the rear of the base.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
